SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.util.heattransfer

Class CompositeWallConduction

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.util.heattransfer.CompositeWallConduction
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class CompositeWallConduction extends Object implements Serializable
One-dimensional transient heat-conduction solver for a multi-layer (composite) vessel wall.

The wall is discretized into a stack of material layers (for example an inner polymer liner, a composite overwrap and an outer steel shell). Each layer is split into a number of control volumes and the resulting non-uniform grid is integrated in time with the unconditionally stable Crank-Nicolson scheme. A tridiagonal system is assembled each step and solved with the Thomas algorithm.

The outer face receives a prescribed incident heat flux (the absorbed fire flux, a Neumann boundary condition) and the inner face exchanges heat with the contained fluid through a convective film coefficient (a Robin boundary condition). Because the solver resolves the temperature gradient across the wall thickness, it captures the through- wall thermal lag that the single-node lumped-capacitance model misses once the Biot number is no longer small. The static biotNumber(double, double, double) helper supports that screening: the lumped model is adequate for Bi < 0.1, and this distributed solver should be preferred above it.

Wetted and unwetted zones of a partially filled vessel are represented by two independent instances driven with the same outer fire flux but with different inner film coefficients and fluid temperatures.

References: Crank and Nicolson (1947), Proc. Camb. Phil. Soc. 43, 50; Patankar (1980), "Numerical Heat Transfer and Fluid Flow"; API 521 §4.3 (fire heat input); BS EN ISO 23251.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • CompositeWallConduction

      public CompositeWallConduction(int nodesPerLayer)
      Creates a composite wall conduction solver.
      Parameters:
      nodesPerLayer - number of control volumes used to discretize each material layer; must be at least two
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if nodesPerLayer is less than two

  • Method Details

    • addLayer

      public CompositeWallConduction addLayer(String name, double thicknessM, double conductivity, double density, double specificHeat)
      Adds a material layer to the wall stack, ordered from the outer (fire-exposed) face inward.
      Parameters:
      name - descriptive layer name; must not be empty
      thicknessM - layer thickness in m; must be positive
      conductivity - thermal conductivity in W/(m·K); must be positive
      density - density in kg/m³; must be positive
      specificHeat - specific heat capacity in J/(kg·K); must be positive
      Returns:
      this solver for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is invalid

    • initialize

      public CompositeWallConduction initialize(double uniformTemperatureK)
      Builds the discretization grid and sets a uniform initial temperature in all nodes.
      Parameters:
      uniformTemperatureK - initial temperature in K applied to every node; must be positive
      Returns:
      this solver for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no layers have been added
      IllegalArgumentException - if uniformTemperatureK is not positive

    • step

      public void step(double dt, double outerFluxWPerM2, double innerFilmCoeff, double innerFluidTempK)
      Advances the wall temperature field by one Crank-Nicolson time step.

      The outer face is driven by the absorbed flux outerFluxWPerM2 (positive into the wall) and the inner face exchanges heat with the contained fluid by convection q = innerFilmCoeff * (fluidT - innerSurfaceT).

      Parameters:
      dt - time step in s; must be positive
      outerFluxWPerM2 - absorbed incident flux on the outer face in W/m² (positive into the wall)
      innerFilmCoeff - inner film heat-transfer coefficient in W/(m²·K); must be non-negative
      innerFluidTempK - contained-fluid temperature in K used for the inner convective boundary; must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the grid has not been initialized
      IllegalArgumentException - if dt, innerFilmCoeff or innerFluidTempK is invalid

    • getTemperaturesK

      public double[] getTemperaturesK()
      Gets the current node temperatures from the outer face to the inner face.
      Returns:
      a copy of the node temperature field in K
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the grid has not been initialized

    • getNodePositionsM

      public double[] getNodePositionsM()
      Gets the node centre positions measured from the outer face.
      Returns:
      a copy of the node positions in m
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the grid has not been initialized

    • getOuterSurfaceTemperatureK

      public double getOuterSurfaceTemperatureK()
      Gets the outer-face (fire-exposed) surface temperature.
      Returns:
      outer-face temperature in K
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the grid has not been initialized

    • getInnerSurfaceTemperatureK

      public double getInnerSurfaceTemperatureK()
      Gets the inner-face (fluid side) surface temperature.
      Returns:
      inner-face temperature in K
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the grid has not been initialized

    • getMeanTemperatureK

      public double getMeanTemperatureK()
      Gets the mass-weighted mean wall temperature.
      Returns:
      mean wall temperature in K
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the grid has not been initialized

    • getTotalThicknessM

      public double getTotalThicknessM()
      Gets the total wall thickness across all layers.
      Returns:
      total thickness in m

    • biotNumber

      public static double biotNumber(double filmCoefficient, double characteristicLengthM, double conductivity)
      Computes the Biot number used to decide whether through-wall conduction must be resolved.

      Bi = h * L / k. The single-node lumped-capacitance approximation is acceptable for Bi < 0.1; otherwise this distributed solver should be used.

      Parameters:
      filmCoefficient - surface heat-transfer coefficient in W/(m²·K); must be positive
      characteristicLengthM - characteristic conduction length in m; must be positive
      conductivity - wall thermal conductivity in W/(m·K); must be positive
      Returns:
      Biot number (dimensionless)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is not positive

    • buildGrid

      private void buildGrid()
      Builds the global non-uniform node grid from the stacked layers.

    • computeCapacityAndConductance

      private void computeCapacityAndConductance(double[] capacity, double[] conductance)
      Computes per-node heat capacities (per unit wall area) and inter-node conductances.
      Parameters:
      capacity - output array of node heat capacities in J/(m²·K)
      conductance - output array of inter-node conductances in W/(m²·K)

    • solveTridiagonal

      private double[] solveTridiagonal(double[] a, double[] b, double[] c, double[] d)
      Solves a tridiagonal linear system with the Thomas algorithm.
      Parameters:
      a - sub-diagonal coefficients (a[0] unused)
      b - diagonal coefficients
      c - super-diagonal coefficients (c[n-1] unused)
      d - right-hand side
      Returns:
      solution vector

    • requireInitialized

      private void requireInitialized()
      Verifies that the grid has been initialized.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if initialize(double) has not been called
